arts.ttl: Everton v Bournemouth, Wigan v Fulham, and more: FA Cup fourth round – live
arts.sum: Follow all the latest updates from Saturday’s football Leyton Orient v Man City – live | Live scores | Mail Dom There is a surprise in the other early FA Cup kickoff, albeit not a dramatic one given that Leeds have fielded a second string against Millwall. Still, few would have backed to be leading 2-0 at Elland Road at the 90-minute mark. Millwall’s place in round five seems secure thanks to Femi Azeez’s double either side of half-time. For Leeds, it will be back to the grindstone in terms of their promotion hunt. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Insistence Everton managerial changes impacted player’s form – Toffees man ‘can do much better’
arts.sum: Vitaliy Mykolenko has featured regularly for Everton this season as he’s currently the only recognised left-back at Goodison Park. The Ukraine international played over 2,000 minutes from 23 games for the Toffees so far. Ashley Young, 39, who has mainly operated as a right-back, is the only cover for the left side of the defence. arts.ttl: “If I had to sign…” – Player explains Bournemouth transfer decision, can’t wait to ‘rub shoulders’ with stars
arts.sum: On deadline day, AFC Bournemouth completed the signing of Eli Junior Kroupi and sent him back on loan to FC Lorient until the end of this season. On Thursday, the 18-year-old’s mother, Sandrine, stated the Cherries presented him a ‘great’ sporting project which convinced him to move. arts.ttl: Everton vs Bournemouth: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Everton are aiming to continue their resurgence in the FA Cup fourth round on Saturday afternoon when they host in-form Bournemouth. Bournemouth have been in similarly impressive form of late and tasted their first defeat since November during last weekend's 2-0 home loss to league leaders Liverpool .
